sourceRef.citation: Kourovka Notebook, Problem 17.84 (Khukhro & Mazurov eds., ed.17)

statement.latex: An associative algebra$A$is said to be Calabi--Yau of dimension$d$(for short, CY$d$) if there is a natural isomorphism of$A$-bimodules${ Ext}_{A }^d(A,\,A A) A$and${ Ext}_{A }^n(A,\,A A)=0$for$n d$. By Kontsevich's theorem, the complex group algebra${ C}G$of the fundamental group$G$of a 3-dimensional aspherical manifold is CY$3$. Is every group with CY$3$complex group algebra residually finite? { ...

reiAssessment.solveRationale: Group theory I_INFINITE_SEARCH; Rei not specialized in group theory (yet)
reiAssessment.formalizationNotes: Kourovka problems are typically research-level group theory
